title: calendula suffruticosa subsp. cinerea
intro: Calendula suffruticosa subsp. cinerea, also known as a subspecies of the pot marigold, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as wounds, burns, and eczema, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Calendula suffruticosa subsp. cinerea can be prepared into various herbal products, including oils, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
